SEARCH
Package server

Class MyHTTPServer

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Thread
server.MyHTTPServer
All Implemented Interfaces:
Runnable, HTTPServer
public class MyHTTPServer extends Thread implements HTTPServer
A multi-threaded HTTP server implementation of HTTPServer that extends Thread.

The server runs its main connection-accepting socket loop on a dedicated background thread, preventing the application's primary execution thread from blocking. Incoming connection requests are delegated to an internal thread pool executor for concurrent processing.

Core Architectural Features

  • Thread Pool Concurrency: Client requests are handled concurrently using a fixed-size thread pool managed by an ExecutorService. This caps the maximum number of active client threads, protecting system resources against client volume spikes.
  • Longest Prefix Routing: Incoming paths are mapped to servlet controllers using the longest-prefix matching algorithm. This enables a registered servlet (e.g., mapped to "/app/") to handle diverse nested URIs and request parameters (e.g., "/app/download/user?id=123").
  • Periodic Shutdown Evaluation: Setting a socket timeout of 1000 milliseconds on ServerSocket ensures that the blocking accept() method wakes up periodically. This allows the server loop to evaluate the state of the volatile running exit flag and shut down cleanly when requested.

Required Core Framework Components (Internal Dependencies):

  • HTTPServer - Main interface specifying server lifecycle controls.
  • Servlet - Controller interface used to resolve requests.
  • RequestParser - Parsing engine that extracts structural request contexts.

Code Integration Example:


 // Start the server listening on port 8080 with 10 worker threads
 MyHTTPServer server = new MyHTTPServer(8080, 10);
 
 // Bind servlet controllers to routes
 server.addServlet("GET", "/publish", new TopicDisplayer());
 server.addServlet("POST", "/upload", new ConfLoader());
 
 // Start background listening
 server.start();
 
 // Close resources when done
 server.close();
See Also:
  • HTTPServer
  • Servlet
  • RequestParser
  • ExecutorService

  • Constructor Details

    • MyHTTPServer

      public MyHTTPServer(int port, int nThreads)
      Initializes the server with a port and thread capacity. Sets up concurrent hash maps to hold registered servlets safely. The port parameter specifies the port number to listen on. The nThreads parameter specifies the maximum number of threads in the pool.
      Parameters:
      port - the local port number to bind the server socket to
      nThreads - the maximum number of background threads in the executor pool

  • Method Details

    • addServlet

      public void addServlet(String httpCommanmd, String uri, Servlet s)
      Registers a servlet to handle requests matching the specified HTTP command and URI route prefix.
      Specified by:
      addServlet in interface HTTPServer
      Parameters:
      httpCommanmd - the targeted HTTP request method (e.g., "GET", "POST", "DELETE")
      uri - the target request URI path prefix
      s - the servlet controller instance to execute
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the provided HTTP command is unsupported

    • removeServlet

      public void removeServlet(String httpCommanmd, String uri)
      Unregisters the servlet bound to the specified HTTP command and URI route prefix.
      Specified by:
      removeServlet in interface HTTPServer
      Parameters:
      httpCommanmd - the targeted HTTP request method to clear
      uri - the mapped request URI path prefix to clear
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the provided HTTP command is unsupported

    • run

      public void run()
      Main execution logic running in a background thread. Listens for client connections and submits them to the thread pool.
      Specified by:
      run in interface Runnable
      Overrides:
      run in class Thread

    • close

      public void close()
      Shuts down the HTTP server. Stops the main loop, closes open resources, and terminates the thread pool executor.
      Specified by:
      close in interface HTTPServer
